summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server-side code inside the app bundle so users only need to install one thing.
summary.outcome: Successfully set up GitHub Actions CI to build and publish macOS DMG releases, created a Homebrew tap (nosman/homebrew-gossamer) with an auto-updating Cask formula, and resolved multiple CI/tooling issues. The app is installable via `brew tap nosman/gossamer && brew install --cask gossamer`, but requires an `xattr -cr` workaround due to missing code signing.

repo: The project has two package.json files: root for the Node.js server, app/ for the Electron frontend — they are currently kept separate intentionally.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths, so the bundled layout inside the .app must preserve the relative directory structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is called at runtime by pushSchema — it must be in production dependencies to survive npm prune.
utilityProcess.fork() (Electron) only accepts CJS entry points; the ESM server needs a CJS wrapper (server-launcher.cjs) that dynamic-imports it.
The homebrew/gossamer.rb template uses sed-substitutable placeholders (GOSSAMER_VERSION, GOSSAMER_ARM64_SHA256, GOSSAMER_X64_SHA256) to allow CI to update it without heredocs.
The live Cask in homebrew-gossamer is auto-updated by the release workflow via HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN; the homebrew/ directory in the main repo is just the template source.

code.finding: Modified to spawn the bundled server via utilityProcess.fork() pointing at Contents/Resources/server/server-launcher.cjs in production; dev mode is gated by presence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL.

Added electron-builder, extraResources config to pull server dist/node_modules/prisma into the .app bundle, and a repository field required by electron-builder when the .git dir is in a parent directory.

Must include permissions: contents: write at job level (GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read-only). Uses --publish never with electron-builder and lets softprops/action-gh-release handle uploads. Syncs app/package.json version from the git tag before building to keep DMG filenames consistent with release URLs.
CJS bridge file created to satisfy utilityProcess.fork()'s CJS requirement; it dynamic-imports the ESM server entry at dist/serve.js.

workflow: GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read-only permissions; any job that creates releases or pushes to the repo must explicitly declare `permissions: contents: write`.
npm ci will fail if package-lock.json is out of sync — adding new deps (e.g. electron-builder) requires running npm install locally first to update the lockfile.
electron-builder needs a `repository` field in package.json when the app directory is not the git root, otherwise it cannot detect the GitHub remote.
electron-builder should be invoked with --publish never when a separate upload step (e.g. softprops/action-gh-release) handles the release assets, otherwise it tries to publish itself and conflicts.
Heredocs inside YAML `run: |` blocks cause YAML parse errors due to indentation; use sed substitution against a tracked template file instead.
Homebrew Cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory in the tap repository.
Fine-grained GitHub PATs only need Contents: Read and write (plus auto-included Metadata: Read) to create releases or push to a tap repo — no other permissions required.
When the CI auto-pushes to a repo (homebrew-gossamer) between local commits, a rebase is needed before pushing local changes to avoid conflicts.

friction: package-lock.json was not updated after adding electron-builder to package.json, causing npm ci to fail in CI with a sync error.
electron-builder could not detect the GitHub repository because .git is in the parent of app/ — required adding an explicit `repository` field to app/package.json.
electron-builder attempted to publish DMGs itself even though softprops was handling uploads, resulting in 'skipped publishing' logs and no artifacts being uploaded.
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN lacked write permissions by default, causing release creation to fail silently.
Version mismatch between git tag (v1.0.7) and hardcoded app/package.json version (1.0.0) caused DMG filenames to not match release download URLs, breaking brew install.
A YAML syntax error from a heredoc inside a `run: |` block broke the workflow and required a rebase/fix push.
Network outage during the session prevented several pushes, requiring commits to be held locally and pushed later.
The CI had already auto-pushed an update to homebrew-gossamer by the time local commits were ready to push, causing rebase conflicts.
macOS Gatekeeper shows 'damaged and can't be opened' for unsigned/unnotarized apps, requiring a manual xattr workaround.
open_items: App is unsigned and unnotarized — proper fix requires an Apple Developer account ($99/yr), a Developer ID Application certificate stored as CI secrets (CSC_LINK, CSC_KEY_PASSWORD), and notarization credentials (APPLE_ID, APPLE_APPSPECIFIC_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID). The current xattr caveat is a stopgap.

The app/ and server code are in separate directories with separate package.json/node_modules — user acknowledged this is suboptimal now that the server is bundled inside the app, but deferred consolidation.
The Homebrew Cask caveats instruct users to run `xattr -cr /Applications/Gossamer.app` — this should be removed once code signing is in place.
